description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) confirmed portfolio VM still builds request-data (including daily-compounded-returns) before signature gating in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/portfolio/vm.cljs (lines ~1131-1143 and ~1197-1205). Reorder pipeline so signature is computed first and short-circuits request-data + daily row derivation when unchanged. Capture before/after timings for repeated renders with unchanged source versions.
title: Compute portfolio metrics request signature before heavy row derivation

acceptance_criteria: 1) /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs becomes a thin facade with no cross-domain business logic. 2) Subdomain namespaces exist for websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault adapters with clear ownership. 3) Existing runtime wiring continues to use stable public vars without behavior regression. 4) Required gates pass: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
description: Refactor /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into bounded subdomain adapter namespaces so runtime seams are easier to reason about and safer for future agent edits. Current evidence: file is 820 LOC (821 with trailing newline) and has 40 require aliases, the highest fan-out in src/hyperopen. It currently mixes websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault workflows in one module. Keep the public namespace hyperopen.runtime.effect-adapters stable during migration while implementation moves behind subdomain modules.
title: Decompose runtime/effect_adapters by subdomain with stable facade

acceptance_criteria: 1) New subdirectory /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters/ exists with a documented module map. 2) Facade namespace still resolves all existing public symbols and call signatures. 3) No behavior change in runtime wiring. 4) Targeted tests for facade export parity are present.
description: Introduce /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters/ namespace tree and move shared helpers (effect-handler wrappers, storage/navigation adapters, shared error helpers) to a small common module. Keep /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs as the stable facade namespace that re-exports all existing public vars used by /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/app/effects.cljs and /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/core/compat.cljs.
title: Create effect-adapters subdomain scaffold and facade export map

acceptance_criteria: 1) Websocket + diagnostics adapter functions are owned by websocket subdomain namespace(s). 2) Behavior and signatures remain identical at facade exports. 3) Existing websocket/runtime tests continue to pass and include at least one regression asserting health diagnostics enrichment parity.
description: Move websocket runtime adapter implementations out of /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into dedicated websocket-focused namespace(s), including init/reconnect/refresh health, active-asset/orderbook/trades/webdata2 subscribe-unsubscribe adapters, diagnostics copy/reset/reveal handlers, and restore-active-asset startup seam. Preserve deterministic effect ordering and health diagnostics payload shape.
title: Extract websocket and diagnostics effect adapters into websocket subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Asset-selector adapter logic lives outside the monolith file in dedicated namespace(s). 2) Runtime behavior remains stable for cache restore/persist and context subscription diffing. 3) Tests cover queue flush scheduling and owner-scoped active-ctx subscription parity.
description: Move asset-selector-specific runtime adapters out of /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into an asset-selector subdomain module. Scope includes icon status queue/flush scheduling, market cache persist/restore, active market display persist/load, and selector-visible active-asset-ctx subscription diffing logic.
title: Extract asset-selector effect adapters into dedicated subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Wallet adapter functions and timeout helpers are isolated in wallet namespace(s). 2) Facade exports remain backward compatible for app/effects wiring. 3) Wallet copy feedback and disconnect cleanup tests remain green with parity assertions.
description: Move wallet runtime adapter logic into wallet-focused namespace(s), including connect/disconnect, storage mode mutation, copy-wallet-address feedback lifecycle, and timeout scheduling/cleanup seams that currently sit in runtime/effect_adapters.cljs. Preserve deterministic UI feedback behavior and runtime timeout ownership boundaries.
title: Extract wallet effect adapters into wallet subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Order adapter wrappers and toast helper pipeline live in dedicated order namespace(s). 2) make-api-* factories preserve signatures used by app/effects runtime wiring. 3) Regression tests verify no behavior change for toast scheduling and API error projection.
description: Move order submission/cancel/position TPSL/margin adapter wrappers and shared order feedback toast helpers out of runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into an order subdomain module. Keep injected collaborator seams explicit (dispatch, runtime error normalization, prune canceled open orders) and preserve toast timing behavior.
title: Extract order effect adapters into order subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Funding adapter implementations are isolated from unrelated domains. 2) Funding predictability loading/success/error behavior stays unchanged. 3) Funding workflow submit/fetch effects remain wired and covered by regression tests.
description: Move funding-related adapter logic from runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into funding namespace(s): active-asset funding predictability state projection flow plus funding workflow API effects (fee estimate, withdrawal queue, transfer, withdraw, deposit). Maintain deterministic loading/error projection semantics and runtime-error normalization.
title: Extract funding effect adapters into funding subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Vault adapter wrappers are isolated in vaults namespace(s). 2) Existing app/effects API map keys continue to resolve through stable facade exports. 3) Vault API effect tests and wiring tests pass without behavior regressions.
description: Move vault-oriented runtime API adapter wrappers from runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into a vaults-focused namespace, including index/summaries/user-equities/detail/webdata2/fills/funding-history/order-history/ledger-updates fetch effects and vault transfer submit effect. Preserve injected collaborator seams and toast/error normalization contracts.
title: Extract vault effect adapters into vaults subdomain module

acceptance_criteria: 1) Runtime effect adapter tests are organized by subdomain (websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, vaults, facade). 2) Facade export contract test protects against accidental public API drift. 3) Full required gates pass: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
description: Refactor /hyperopen/test/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters_test.cljs into subdomain-oriented test namespaces that mirror the new runtime/effect_adapters/* module boundaries. Add explicit facade parity tests that ensure /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs continues exporting the required stable public API used by app/effects and core/compat macros.
title: Split effect adapter tests by subdomain and add facade parity contract coverage

description: Refactor /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/funding/actions.cljs and /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs into explicit domain, application, and infrastructure boundaries while preserving runtime action/effect contracts and funding modal behavior.
notes:
Implemented first refactor slice: funding.actions is now a facade over funding.application.modal-actions; extracted lifecycle normalization to funding.domain.lifecycle; extracted HyperUnit request/fallback client to funding.infrastructure.hyperunit-client; funding.effects now consumes domain/infrastructure seams. Required gates currently pass (npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket). Remaining: split modal view-model/action orchestration and effects submit/polling orchestration into smaller application modules.
2026-03-03: Completed route-client infrastructure extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/route_clients.cljs for LiFi/Across URL construction + payload parsing, rewired funding/effects.cljs via compatibility wrappers, and validated with npm run check, npm test, and npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Completed wallet-RPC infrastructure extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/wallet_rpc.cljs (provider request, chain switch, tx receipt polling, send+confirm helper), rewired funding/effects.cljs through private compatibility aliases, and re-ran npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Completed submit-orchestration application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/submit_effects.cljs for transfer/withdraw/deposit submit flows and converted funding/effects.cljs entrypoints to compatibility wrappers with preserved defaults. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed lifecycle-polling application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/lifecycle_polling.cljs for polling orchestration and rewired funding/effects.cljs polling entrypoints to compatibility wrappers with injected seams. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed modal view-model application split. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_vm.cljs and replaced modal_actions funding-modal-view-model with a compatibility wrapper that injects existing helper seams. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed modal command split + HyperUnit address client extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_commands.cljs and rewired modal_actions command entrypoints as compatibility wrappers. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/hyperunit_address_client.cljs and rewired funding/effects.cljs HyperUnit address request/fallback helpers through compatibility wrappers. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed ERC20 RPC infrastructure extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/infrastructure/erc20_rpc.cljs for calldata encoding and eth_call balance/allowance reads; rewired funding/effects.cljs to use compatibility aliases/wrappers. Validation: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all green.
2026-03-03: Completed domain-policy and deposit-transaction extraction slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/domain/policy.cljs for modal parsing/preview/fee-display/lifecycle status policy and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/application/modal_actions.cljs as a thin compatibility/composition facade. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/deposit_submit.cljs for Bridge2/LiFi/Across deposit transaction orchestration and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs through dependency-injected wrappers. Validation: npx shadow-cljs compile app, npx shadow-cljs compile test, npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket all passed.
2026-03-03: Completed HyperUnit query orchestration extraction. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/hyperunit_query.cljs and rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs wrappers for existing-address selection/prefetch plus fee/withdrawal-queue fetch flows, preserving private-var test seams. Validation rerun: npx shadow-cljs compile app, npx shadow-cljs compile test, npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
2026-03-03: Committed refactor slices as 1d7d18f and updated ExecPlan. Remote landing remains blocked in this environment: git pull/push failed with GitHub SSH auth (publickey), and bd dolt push has no configured remote origin.
2026-03-03: Completed optional final lifecycle-helper cleanup slice. Added src/hyperopen/funding/application/lifecycle_guards.cljs for modal activity + poll-token guard ownership and src/hyperopen/funding/domain/lifecycle_operations.cljs for operation selection, lifecycle conversion, and polling delay policy. Rewired src/hyperopen/funding/effects.cljs through compatibility wrappers/aliases and reduced file size to ~834 LOC. Validation rerun: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket (all green).
title: Funding bounded-context split (domain/application/infrastructure)

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found the position overlay repaint path rebuilding overlay DOM on every repaint in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/utils/chart_interop/position_overlays.cljs (clear-children! + rebuild at lines ~758-813). Recent overlay chip additions increased per-repaint node/style work. Implement incremental overlay updates (reuse existing row/chip nodes and only patch coordinates/text/styles on change), then measure scripting time and node mutation count under websocket/timescale churn.
notes: Implemented in /Users/barry/.codex/worktrees/19fa/hyperopen: position_overlays now reuses cached PNL/liquidation DOM rows and patches styles/text/coordinates in place without root clear+rebuild per repaint. Added regression test position-overlays-reuses-dom-rows-across-overlay-updates-test to assert node identity reuse and content updates. Validation passed: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
title: Reduce chart position overlay repaint DOM churn

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found O(n log n) sort work in render-triggered path: latest-entry-fill sorts matching fills each build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/utils/position_overlay_model.cljs (lines ~113-119), called from trading chart overlay model build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/trading_chart/core.cljs (lines ~398-409). Pre-index latest entry fill per asset/side (or memoize by stable fill source version) to remove repeated sort cost on chart updates.
title: Remove per-render fill sort from chart position overlay model

description: Implement Trade.xyz-style Ghost Mode in Hyperopen: spectate any address while preserving signer safety and deterministic runtime behavior. Scope includes account-context architecture seam, websocket/API address routing, UI entry/exit controls, read-only trading guardrails, watchlist+pinned addresses, and full regression coverage.
notes:
2026-03-04: Milestone 2 completed. Migrated read-side runtime/websocket/account-history/projection freshness routing to effective-account-address, added Ghost-mode effective-address regression coverage, and reran validation gates (npm test, npm run check, npm run test:websocket) all green.
2026-03-04: Milestone 3 completed. Added Ghost Mode action domain + runtime wiring, header entry controls, app-level active spectating banner with explicit stop control, modal/watchlist UI, and regression coverage in ghost_mode_actions/header_view/app_shell tests. Validation gates rerun and green (npm test, npm run check, npm run test:websocket).
title: Ghost Mode account spectating

description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) found funding comparison VM rebuilding parse/filter/sort on each VM build in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/funding_comparison/vm.cljs (lines ~251-257). Add memoization keyed by predicted-fundings identity/version + query + timeframe + sort + favorites signature so unchanged inputs skip full row pipeline. Include regression test or benchmark-style assertion for large market sets.
title: Add memoized cache for funding comparison parse/filter/sort pipeline
